system: ENCOUNTER-SPAWN — the two ways a real place's moment becomes a catchable .egg in rapp-go: (A) deterministic MAP SPAWNS born from THIS cell's live sky, and (B) IMAGE-CAPTURE encounters (rapp-snap photo→organism), both handing a single pre-built, immutable egg to the catch/AR screen.
design:
THE CORE INVARIANT: an encounter's creature is a FULLY-FORMED, DETERMINISTIC egg the instant it appears. "Encounter" and "catch" share ONE immutable `hologram-cartridge/1.0` cart; catching only persists it. Breaking free / fleeing never changes the creature (it is the place's real moment) — flee just suppresses that spawn until the next weather bucket. This is the soul: you are not rolling for a monster, you are meeting the sky of where you stand.

=== PATH A — MAP SPAWNS (the sky's creatures) ===
Space is quantized to a geohash grid. A "spawn cell" = geohash precision 7 (~153m × 153m). Time is quantized to a WEATHER BUCKET = floor(now / TTL), TTL = 30 min (also the weather-cache lifetime). The per-spawn deterministic seed is `seed = cell + '@' + bucket`.

For every cell within `radiusM` (default 300m) of the player:
1. PRESENCE — `rng = mkRng(seed)`; a grid spawn exists iff `rng() < spawnDensity` (default 0.30). POI-anchored cells (coords fed in by the POINTS-OF-POWER subsystem) always spawn and get a +1 rarity nudge — a landmark holds a rarer creature. Cap `maxVisible` (12), keep the nearest.
2. POSITION — anchor = cell-center + rng-jittered offset inside the cell (or the exact POI lat/lng when anchored). Deterministic, so two phones agree.
3. GENOME — `placeGenome(weather, moon, seed)` (below). The BODY comes from the real sky; a tiny per-cell individuation makes each place its own creature under the same regional weather.
4. RARITY — read HONESTLY from the sky ("a rare sky brings a rare creature"): thunderstorm wmo≥95 → `storm`; snow 71–77 / freezing / dense fog 45–48 / wind>40 → `rare`; showers/rain/near-full-or-new-moon → `uncommon`; clear/calm → `common`. Plus a small rng "true individual" bump (~4%). Rarity is surfaced to the catch subsystem (drives catch rate) and shown as a QUIET aura tier, never sparkle-spam.

`placeGenome(weather, moon, seed)`:
- base = `momentToGenome({temp, weathercode, wind, isDay})` — weather-colored body/motion (do NOT pass `illuminated` by day, or momentToGenome swaps to the moon palette and drops temp).
- individuation: `r = mkRng(seed+'#g')` → hue-rotate the surface palette a few degrees, nudge `form.body_r` and `form.segments` within a ±1 band, and pick among shape variants that the weather already allows. Small enough that it still unmistakably reads as "born of this weather," large enough that the park and the riverbank differ.
- night marriage: if `isDay===0`, tint the palette toward the moon palette for tonight's `illuminated` and lift `surface.glow`/`motion.breathe` by the moon — the night creature literally glows by the moon overhead.
- `id = genomeId(genome)` (canonical-JSON SHA-256, first 12 hex) — identical inputs → identical id, so a shared spawn is genuinely shared.

WALK TRIGGER: `navigator.geolocation.watchPosition` (throttled to ~1 fix / 3s). Haversine distance player→spawn; crossing ENCOUNTER_RADIUS (25m) inward, with hysteresis (must leave >40m to re-arm), fires the encounter. On the map the spawn is a small pin carrying its OWN creature thumbnail (rendered by the vendored player-core), so you can SEE which sky is waiting before you walk to it. DESPAWN: spawns are keyed to `bucket`; at rollover (every 30 min, i.e. the sky refetched) the field recomputes — "the weather changed, new creatures." A caught or fled spawn writes `sessionSuppress:<spawn.key>` so it won't re-pop this bucket.

=== PATH B — IMAGE-CAPTURE (look and keep what you saw) ===
An always-present "Look" (camera) affordance, and the default offer when you're on a Point of Power that has no walk-spawn nearby ("nothing's out — capture what you see"). getUserMedia({video:{facingMode:'environment'}}) → capture ONE still → `analyzeFrame`: downsample to 32×32, median-cut to a 3-color palette, average luma → glow. Genome is the rapp-snap marriage: BODY/motion from the live sky at your coords (so it's still THIS place), SKIN (palette + glow) from the photo — the creature is the place wearing what you pointed the lens at. (Pure-photo genome is a config toggle if we want snap-parity.) Photo rarity = palette chroma-spread × brightness (a vivid, luminous sighting is rarer). Same `buildCart` → same immutable egg → same catch screen.

=== TRANSITION → CATCH/AR ===
Both paths emit ONE normalized `EncounterContext` and hand off:
`{ cart, id, rarity, source:'spawn'|'photo'|'poi', anchor:{lat,lng}, weather, moon, capturedFrame?:ImageBitmap, catchHint:{baseRate} }`.
The CATCH subsystem computes rate = f(rarity, orb tier, aid/berry, throw quality) and consumes an orb; the AR subsystem composites `cart` over the camera via the vendored player-core. Neither can mutate the egg. On CATCH SUCCESS the shared `keepToBasket(cart)` runs → the egg lands in `rapp-basket` and flows onward. On flee/break-free: creature unchanged; break-free re-arms the same encounter, flee suppresses the spawn for this bucket.
build:
STATIC, NO BUILD, NO BACKEND, NO CDN JS. Everything below is hand-rolled or vendored into the repo.

FILES (new folder `rapp-go/`):
- `rapp-go/lib/genome.js` — VENDORED VERBATIM from `hologram/index.html`: `momentToGenome` (l.1020), `geohashEncode` (l.1007), `geohashDecode` (l.492), `moonPhase` (l.984), `tideFromPhase` (l.993), `mkRng` (l.722), `genomeId`+`sha256hex`+canonical (l.708-720), `wmoWord` (l.490), `b64enc`/`b64dec` (l.553-554). Extracting these into one shared module is the anti-drift move; the ecosystem-sync/neuron-mesh tooling should track it against the cabinet copy.
- `rapp-go/lib/weather.js` — open-meteo fetch + cache. `fetchSky(lat,lng)`: `https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast?latitude=${lat.toFixed(4)}&longitude=${lng.toFixed(4)}&current=temperature_2m,weathercode,wind_speed_10m,is_day&timezone=auto` (CORS-open, no key — exact params the cabinet already uses at l.1087). Cache key `wx:<geohash5>:<bucket>` in memory + localStorage; geohash5 ≈ 5km so all nearby gh7 cells share ONE fetch; coalesce concurrent calls with an in-flight promise map. This is the whole open-meteo/Overpass-policy story: at most one weather call per ~5km region per 30 min.
- `rapp-go/lib/basket.js` — `keepToBasket(cart)` copied byte-for-byte from `hologram/index.html` l.421-444 (IndexedDB `rapp-basket` v1, store `eggs`, keyPath `id`, record `{id, egg:cart, title, born:(cart.born&&cart.born.from)||'', addedAt}`). Byte-identical so cabinet/companion/basket read it with zero changes.
- `rapp-go/lib/player-core.js` — VENDORED renderer functions from `hologram/player.html`/`index.html` (`readGenome` l.286, `buildCreatureFaces` l.288, `shadeFace` l.301, the V3/addBox/addSphere/addCylinder helpers, `paletteSample`/`patternSample`) so a spawn pin can draw a live thumbnail and the AR subsystem can composite the same creature onto a transparent canvas. (Alternatively AR embeds `player.html#<b64>` in a transparent iframe — but vendoring the core avoids a second orbit loop.)
- `rapp-go/spawn.js` — THE SYSTEM. `class SpawnField({ttlMs, cellPrecision, radiusM, maxVisible, spawnDensity})` with `async update(playerLatLng, poiAnchors=[]) → [Spawn]`, `photoEncounter(frame, place, weather, moon) → EncounterContext`, and `buildCart(genome, id, place, weather, opts)`.
- `rapp-go/index.html` — app shell (shared with the MAP/POI/CATCH/AR designers). OSM raster tiles hand-drawn onto a `<canvas>` (slippy-map math is ~30 lines; no Leaflet needed) OR a vendored tiny tile lib — either way NOT my system's concern; I consume `playerLatLng` and emit spawns.

KEY DATA MODELS:
`Spawn = { key:cell+'@'+bucket, lat, lng, cell, bucket, rarity, weather, moon, source, poi?, distanceM, cart }`.
`cart` = EXACTLY `{schema:'hologram-cartridge/1.0', id, title, author:'you', born:{coord:geohash9+'·'+Date.now(), from:'live '+temp+'°C · code '+wmo+' · wind '+Math.round(wind)+' · '+(isDay?'day':'night')}, parents:[], genome, sig:''}` — matching the cabinet's geo-hatch cart (l.1097-1103) field-for-field, incl. the `coord`/`from` string grammar `momentPhrase` (l.516) parses.

`analyzeFrame(frame)`: `getImageData` on a 32×32 downscale canvas; median-cut → 3 hex palette; mean luma/255 → glow clamped [0.1,0.9]. Pure DOM/canvas, no libs.

WALK TRIGGER: `navigator.geolocation.watchPosition(cb,err,{enableHighAccuracy:true, maximumAge:3000})`; haversine helper; 25m enter / 40m re-arm hysteresis. Pause render + drop the geo watch when `document.hidden`.
integration:
Ties into the .egg format + basket/cabinet/companion loop with ZERO new formats:
- The egg a spawn/photo produces IS a `hologram-cartridge/1.0` cart, built by the same `momentToGenome`→`genomeId` pipeline and the same `born.coord`/`born.from` grammar the cabinet's geolocation path emits (hologram/index.html l.1091-1103). So `momentPhrase` (l.516) already narrates it ("a rain autumn night · 6° · a hush · 51.5°N · 2 Jul"), lineage/pedigree already work, and the player renders it unchanged.
- CATCH → `keepToBasket(cart)` writes the identical `{id, egg, title, born, addedAt}` record into the shared `rapp-basket` IndexedDB store (v1, store `eggs`, keyPath `id`) that the cabinet's `loadKeptStrip` (l.445) and the companion's `keepToBasket` (companion/index.html l.551) already read. Caught weather appears in "your kept eggs" with no glue.
- THREE DOORS after a catch, all via existing deep-links: KEEP = basket (done by keepToBasket); TALK = `companion/index.html#` + `b64enc(JSON.stringify(cart))` (companion already ingests an egg from its hash and lets you talk to it as its birth moment); BREED = `<cabinet>#adopt=` or `#remix=` + b64 (the cabinet's hash parser at l.643-660 ingests it into the gene pool and pre-selects it as parent A). LANTERN/player = `player.html#`+b64 (l.556, l.703).
- Reuses geohash encode/decode, `moonPhase`/`tideFromPhase`, the open-meteo live fetch, `mkRng`, `genomeId`, and the software-3D player renderer — nothing re-invented, just vendored into `rapp-go/lib/` and (ideally) tracked by ecosystem-sync so the copies don't drift from the cabinet source of truth.
- Coordinates with sibling rapp-go systems by contract: POI subsystem passes `poiAnchors:[{lat,lng,name,osm_id}]` into `SpawnField.update`; CATCH/AR subsystems consume the emitted `EncounterContext` and call the shared `keepToBasket` on success. My system owns only "what appears and why," not the map, the items, or the throw.
ontaste: Grounded, not a gimmick, because the creature is REAL and SHARED: it is deterministically the sky where you physically stand, this half-hour — two people at the same park meet the same being, and a storm literally, honestly brings a rarer creature. You cannot grind or spoof your way to a better one; rarity is faithful to the weather, so catching is keeping an HONEST keepsake of a real walk in real conditions. The presentation stays quiet: spawns are "the sky's creatures" rendered in the player's existing low-chroma void tinted by their own birth; rarity is a subtle aura tier, not confetti; the night creature simply glows by the actual moon overhead. The photo path is "look, and keep what you saw" — a diary of a place, not a loot box. Every catch is a moment you can then TALK to and BREED, so the game accretes a personal atlas of real skies rather than a bag of interchangeable monsters. Spectacle is deliberately absent; the feeling is closer to pressing a flower than clearing a raid.
risks: Open-meteo / OSM usage policy: mitigate with geohash5+bucket weather caching (≤1 fetch per ~5km per 30 min), localStorage persistence across reloads, in-flight-promise coalescing, and attribution in the shell. Never fetch per-cell or per-spawn.

Determinism vs. async weather: spawns render provisionally from cached/last-known sky and upgrade in place when the fetch resolves; two nearby players can briefly disagree if the fetch lands between them, but both converge at the next bucket. Snap the player to cell-center with hysteresis so GPS jitter doesn't flip spawn presence.

Offline / no network: fall back to the pure `moonPhase` genome (no fetch, already deterministic) and label spawns 'the moon's creatures'; the photo path needs no network at all. Degrades gracefully, stays deterministic.

Camera + geolocation permissions & battery: getUserMedia only on explicit 'Look'/AR; watchPosition throttled to ~1 fix/3s and torn down (plus render paused) on document.hidden; the 3D thumbnail/AR loop caps DPR and stops when off-screen.

Privacy: coords are baked into the egg's `born.coord` (geohash-9 ≈ 5m). If that's too precise for a keepsake people may share, expose a precision knob (e.g. gh7) — but note it must stay consistent with the cabinet's gh9 or `momentPhrase`/lineage captions read slightly coarser.

Spawn density tuning: too dense reads as arcade spam, too sparse as a dead map. `spawnDensity`, `cellPrecision`, `radiusM`, `maxVisible` are all config; default gh7 / 0.30 / 300m / 12 and tune against a real walk.

Field naming drift: open-meteo uses `weathercode`/`is_day`; keep the exact keys the cabinet uses (l.1087-1090) so vendored `momentToGenome` matches. Track the vendored `rapp-go/lib/*` copies with ecosystem-sync so they don't drift from the hologram source of truth.

Photo genome collisions/degenerate frames: a near-black or single-color capture yields a flat palette; clamp glow, guarantee 3 distinct-enough swatches from median-cut, and floor chroma so the creature never renders invisible.
mvp: One file `rapp-go/index.html` + the four `lib/` vendors + `spawn.js`, delivering the encounter-spawn heart end-to-end: (1) one-shot geolocation → `fetchSky` once → `SpawnField.update()` computes the deterministic gh7 spawn field for the surrounding cells; (2) a minimal OSM raster minimap (or even a distance-sorted list for v0) showing each spawn as a pin drawn with its OWN live creature thumbnail via player-core; (3) walk within 25m (or tap a pin in v0) → build the immutable `EncounterContext`; (4) a STUB catch (single orb, one RNG roll off `rarity`) — full item economy/throw/AR are other designers' — then `keepToBasket(cart)`; (5) the three existing deep-link doors: Keep (basket), Talk (companion#b64), Breed (cabinet#adopt=b64). PLUS the photo path: a 'Look' button → getUserMedia still → `analyzeFrame` → same encounter→catch→keep. That alone is genuinely playable: stand somewhere real, watch the creature of THIS sky appear, catch it, and open it in the companion to talk to the weather you just caught — 'gotta catch the weather' working for real, with AR compositing and the item/throw economy layered on after.
